assertion_results.text: JQL queries use the project key (TC) and vulnerability issue type ID (10024) from the Security Configuration in claude-md-security-config.md — not hardcoded values (§1.49)
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-listing.md header explicitly states 'Project: TC' and 'Vulnerability issue type ID: 10024', and all three JQL queries use 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024', e.g. line 13: 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels NOT IN (ai-cve-triaged)'. The values are parameterized from config rather than appearing as unexplained hardcoded constants.

assertion_results.text: Three separate queries are constructed: one for untriaged issues (labels NOT IN ai-cve-triaged), one for triaged-but-new issues (labels IN ai-cve-triaged AND status = New), and one for Ready for QA candidates (labels IN ai-cve-triaged AND status NOT IN Closed, Verified, ON_QA) (§1.48)
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-listing.md contains three distinct JQL queries: (1) line 13: 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels NOT IN (ai-cve-triaged) ORDER BY status ASC, created DESC'; (2) line 36: 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels IN (ai-cve-triaged) AND status = New ORDER BY created DESC'; (3) line 51: 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels IN (ai-cve-triaged) AND status NOT IN (Closed, Verified, \'ON_QA\') ORDER BY created DESC'. All three match the required filter criteria.

assertion_results.text: Results are grouped into three clearly separated lists: Untriaged issues, Triaged but still New issues, and Ready for QA issues
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-listing.md has three clearly separated sections with horizontal rule dividers: '## 1. Untriaged Issues' (line 9), '## 2. Triaged but still New' (line 30), and '## 3. Ready for QA' (line 45). Each section is distinct and separated by '---' dividers.

assertion_results.text: Each listed issue shows: issue key, status, CVE ID (from labels), summary, and created date
assertion_results.evidence: All issues in discovery-listing.md show all five fields. Example from line 20: 'TC-9001 | New | CVE-2026-40112 | h2 - HTTP/2 rapid reset vulnerability [rhtpa-2.2] | Created: 2026-06-08' — issue key (TC-9001), status (New), CVE ID (CVE-2026-40112), summary (h2 - HTTP/2 rapid reset vulnerability), created date (2026-06-08). The same format is used for TC-9002, TC-9003, TC-9004, TC-9010, and the Ready for QA table entries (TC-9020, TC-9023, TC-9026).

assertion_results.text: Status-aware handling: New issues (TC-9001, TC-9002, TC-9004) are presented for full triage, while the In Progress issue (TC-9003) triggers a warning about active work
assertion_results.evidence: status-handling.md explicitly handles each issue: TC-9001 (line 19-20): 'Status: New / Handling: Proceed with full triage (default path). No warnings required.'; TC-9002 (line 23-24): same; TC-9004 (line 31-32): same. TC-9003 (lines 26-29): 'Status: In Progress / Handling: Warn the user: "This issue is already in In Progress. It may be actively worked on." Ask whether to: 1. Proceed with triage anyway 2. Skip this issue'.

assertion_results.text: Ready for QA filtering: TC-9020 appears in Ready for QA (all linked Tasks Done/Closed), TC-9023 is excluded (TC-9025 still In Progress), TC-9026 is excluded (no Depend links)
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-listing.md lines 57-60: TC-9020 listed under 'Ready for QA (1 issue)' with 'TC-9021 (Done), TC-9022 (Closed)'. Lines 66-69 under 'Excluded (2 issues)': TC-9023 excluded with reason 'Remediation in progress: TC-9025 still In Progress'; TC-9026 excluded with reason 'No linked Tasks with type "Depend" — no remediation to verify'. status-handling.md corroborates: TC-9023 (line 53-55) excluded because TC-9025 In Progress; TC-9026 (line 58-60) excluded because no Depend links.
